mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail and food service sales below the prior 3-month average signals weakening consumer demand that compresses earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS declines > 1.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production index below 104.0 combined with rising unemployment signals supply-side weakness and demand destruction that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO < 104.0 AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window. Mechanism fails if INDPRO and UNRATE move in opposite directions in > 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts above 1,250 thousand units combined with rising retail sales signals strong demand momentum and forward economic growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window. Mechanism fails if HOUST and RSAFS diverge in > 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index above 0.4% combined with elevated federal funds rates above 4.0% signals persistent inflation and monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and forward returns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window. Mechanism fails if inflation and rates move in opposite directions in > 35% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 in the prior quarter above 2.5% annualized combined with rising payroll employment signals sustained economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND PAYEMS growth > 100,000 MoM
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with synchronized growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window. Mechanism fails if GDP and employment growth diverge in > 40% of observations.
